title: Parimala Ranganatha Perumal Temple
tagline: Parimala Ranganatha Perumal Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

heroImage.alt: Parimala Ranganatha Perumal Temple, Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Parimala Ranganathar Perumal Temples or Tiruindaloor is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu, located in Thiruvilandur of Mayiladuthurai, a town in the South Indian state of Tamil Na
overview.description: Parimala Ranganathar Perumal Temples or Tiruindaloor is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu, located in Thiruvilandur of Mayiladuthurai, a town in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is one of the Divya Desams, the 108 temples of Vishnu revered in Nalayira Divya Prabandham by the 12 poet saints, or the Alvars. This temple is along Kaveri and is one of the Pancharanga Kshetrams. The temple is believed to be of significant antiquity with contributions at different times from Medieval Cholas, Vijayanagara Empire and Madurai Nayaks.

topPlaces.name: Mayiladuthurai
topPlaces.description: Mayiladuthurai (formerly known as Mayavaram or Mayuram) is a Special Grade Municipality and the district headquarters of Mayiladuthurai district in Tamil Nadu, India. The town is located 256 km (159 mi) from the state capital, Chennai.

topPlaces.name: Koorai silk sari
topPlaces.description: Koorai silk sari, also referred to as Koorai pattu pudavai, Koorai pattu, or Koranad cloth, is a traditional nine-yard sari originating from Koranad in Mayiladuthurai, Tamil Nadu, India. Traditionally worn by brides during Hindu wedding ceremonies, the sari is recognized for its unique weaving patterns and cultural significance.

topPlaces.name: Koorainadu Punukeeswarar Temple
topPlaces.description: Koorainadu Punukeeswarar Temple, or simply Punukeeswarar Temple, is a Hindu temple located in Koorainadu (also known as Koranad) in the town of Mayiladuthurai, Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is dedicated to Lord Punukeeswarar, a form of Shiva.

topPlaces.name: Mayuranathaswami Temple, Mayiladuthurai
topPlaces.description: Mayuranathaswamy Temple, or simply Mayuranathar Temple, is a Hindu temple in the town of Mayiladuthurai (formerly known as Mayavaram or Mayuram) in Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is dedicated to Lord Mayuranathaswamy, a form of Shiva, and has given its name to the town itself.

topPlaces.name: Koranad
topPlaces.description: Koranad (formerly known as Koorainadu ) is an affluent commercial and residential neighbourhood in Mayiladuthurai, Tamil Nadu, India. It is surrounded by Kamarajar colony in the North, Dharmapuram in the East, Srinivasapuram in the South, and Sithkarkadu and Sholampettai in the West.

topPlaces.name: Mayiladuthurai new bus station
topPlaces.description: Mayiladuthurai New Bus Station, officially known as Kamarajar New Bus Station, is a bus terminus, serving the town of Mayiladuthurai, Tamil Nadu, India. The station is named after K. Kamarajar, a former chief minister of Tamil Nadu , who was popularly called Karmaveerar (Great leader).

topPlaces.name: Mayiladuthurai dry fish market
topPlaces.description: Mayiladuthurai dry fish market, also known as the Karuvattu Sandhai or Mayavaram Karuvattu Sandhai, is a dedicated dry fish market operating in town Mayiladuthurai, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Agarakkirangudi
topPlaces.description: Agarakkirangudi is a village in the Mayiladuthurai taluk of Mayiladuthurai district, Tamil Nadu, India.

items.note: Mayiladuthurai (formerly known as Mayavaram or Mayuram) is a Special Grade Municipality and the district headquarters of Mayiladuthurai district in Tamil Nadu, India. The town is located 256 km (159 mi) from the state capital, Chennai.

Koorai silk sari, also referred to as Koorai pattu pudavai, Koorai pattu, or Koranad cloth, is a traditional nine-yard sari originating from Koranad in Mayiladuthurai, Tamil Nadu, India. Traditionally worn by brides during Hindu wedding ceremonies, the sari is recognized for its unique weaving patterns and cultural significance.

Koorainadu Punukeeswarar Temple, or simply Punukeeswarar Temple, is a Hindu temple located in Koorainadu (also known as Koranad) in the town of Mayiladuthurai, Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is dedicated to Lord Punukeeswarar, a form of Shiva.

Mayuranathaswamy Temple, or simply Mayuranathar Temple, is a Hindu temple in the town of Mayiladuthurai (formerly known as Mayavaram or Mayuram) in Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is dedicated to Lord Mayuranathaswamy, a form of Shiva, and has given its name to the town itself.

Koranad (formerly known as Koorainadu ) is an affluent commercial and residential neighbourhood in Mayiladuthurai, Tamil Nadu, India. It is surrounded by Kamarajar colony in the North, Dharmapuram in the East, Srinivasapuram in the South, and Sithkarkadu and Sholampettai in the West.

Mayiladuthurai New Bus Station, officially known as Kamarajar New Bus Station, is a bus terminus, serving the town of Mayiladuthurai, Tamil Nadu, India. The station is named after K. Kamarajar, a former chief minister of Tamil Nadu , who was popularly called Karmaveerar (Great leader).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Parimala Ranganatha Perumal Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Parimala Ranganatha Perumal Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Parimala Ranganatha Perumal Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (109 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (110 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Parimala Ranganatha Perumal Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Parimala Ranganatha Perumal Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Parimala Ranganatha Perumal Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Parimala Ranganatha Perumal Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Parimala Ranganatha Perumal Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mayiladuthurai, Koorai silk sari, Koorainadu Punukeeswarar Temple, Mayuranathaswami Temple, Mayiladuthurai, Koranad.
